{"step_by_step_thinking": "Anaphylaxis is a severe, potentially life-threatening allergic reaction that typically occurs immediately after exposure to an allergen. It is characterized by symptoms such as hives, swelling, difficulty breathing, and a drop in blood pressure. Delayed reactions are not characteristic of anaphylaxis. Passive transfer by serum can occur in anaphylaxis, where antibodies from the serum of an immune individual can transfer to another individual, causing an allergic reaction. Therefore, the delayed reaction is not a feature of anaphylaxis.", "answer_choice": "A"}
